title: Beware My Cheating Bart

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, but low originality since it's overused

jokes.text: Happy Little Elves Two: The Sequelf
jokes.setup: Homer looking for appropriate children's movies
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous sequel title combining 'sequel' and 'elf'
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau that satirizes lazy sequel naming conventions

jokes.text: That movie's for babies. Eh, watch whatever you want; I got you here alive.
jokes.punchline: Homer's extremely low parenting standards - 'got you here alive'
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Homer's negligent parenting style with dark humor

jokes.text: Now I'm gonna visit the only court I could never be in contempt of: food court!
jokes.setup: Homer leaving the boys at movies
jokes.punchline: Wordplay on 'court' - legal vs. food court, implying he's often in legal trouble
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that reveals character backstory about legal troubles

jokes.text: Now, to save on calories, I won't eat the paper in the fortune cookie.
jokes.setup: Homer at food court trying to be health-conscious
jokes.punchline: Absurd implication that he normally eats fortune cookie paper
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor showing Homer's twisted logic about health

jokes.text: Sir, I can't help but noticing that you are very near death. Closer than you think.
jokes.setup: Salesman making observation about Homer's health
jokes.punchline: Homer casually agreeing he's near death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about mortality with Homer's characteristic obliviousness

jokes.text: Homer, how long do you want to live? Long enough that me and Marge can be one of those couples who just sit and hold hands.
jokes.punchline: Homer's unexpectedly sweet and modest aspiration
jokes.explanation: Touching moment that subverts expectations with genuine sweetness

jokes.text: I heard Lenny had a terrible fall. You know who else fell? Dr. Hibbert. Yeah, falls'll getcha.
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre logical chain about falls being epidemic
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's tendency to find patterns where none exist

jokes.text: For money. This is the Butt-Whisperer 9000.
jokes.setup: Salesman offering to help Homer live longer
jokes.punchline: Blunt admission it's for money plus ridiculous treadmill name
jokes.explanation: Funny product name that parodies fitness equipment marketing

jokes.text: Run, you maggot! Faster, you tub of lard! It's programmed to my specifications!
jokes.punchline: Salesman proud that the insults are personalized to him
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about personalized fitness motivation

jokes.text: Homer, you can't afford $300 a month? No. Well, what about ten dollars a day? Easy!
jokes.punchline: Homer falling for obvious math trick ($300/month = $10/day)
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer stupidity with perfect setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: How was Crappy Little Elves? My friend and I only see it to rank on it.
jokes.punchline: Callback to earlier title with Bart's cool excuse for seeing kid movie
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Bart trying to maintain credibility

jokes.text: Crawlspace, based on Paxing kongjian
jokes.setup: Describing horror movie they want to see
jokes.punchline: Satirizing how Hollywood remakes foreign films with pretentious credits
jokes.explanation: Smart satire of Hollywood remake culture

jokes.text: if we don't watch movies about torture in crawl spaces, how will we know what to do if someone puts us in a torture crawl space? Not if, when.
jokes.punchline: Escalation from 'if' to 'when' someone tortures them
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good escalation showing teenage paranoia/bravado

jokes.text: one of those Jennifer Aniston movies where she rolls her eyes on the poster
jokes.setup: Shauna choosing alternative to horror movie
jokes.punchline: Specific observation about Jennifer Aniston's movie poster poses
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific observation that perfectly captures rom-com marketing

jokes.text: You're a total perv magnet.
jokes.setup: Jimbo not wanting Shauna to go alone
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of his girlfriend as attracting perverts
jokes.explanation: Shows Jimbo's crude way of expressing concern

jokes.text: No starter stache, temp tat, still has baby teeth at nine and 24.
jokes.punchline: Detailed inventory of Bart's non-threatening features, implying Jimbo has both at 24
jokes.explanation: Funny itemization that reveals Jimbo's own insecurities

jokes.text: Total pre-puber.
jokes.punchline: Crude but accurate summation
jokes.explanation: Direct character voice but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Come on, come on. The best murder's always first.
jokes.setup: Rushing to get into the movie
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to murder as entertainment with movie wisdom
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing character's casual attitude toward violence

jokes.text: All right, we've seen his ass. Let's go.
jokes.punchline: Shauna's blunt dismissal after brief nudity
jokes.explanation: Shows character's crude directness and short attention span

jokes.text: Hey, I can hook you up with pinto and black beans. Oh, I'll do anything for free beans. Anything.
jokes.punchline: Bart's desperate enthusiasm for free beans
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bart's priorities and desperation

jokes.text: Corn, flour, corn, corn, no gluten, corn. Two stuck together?! Oh, now I got to fill out a form!
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic overreaction to minor tortilla problem
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation satirizing workplace bureaucracy

jokes.text: Looks like two snakes trying to kill each other on your head.
jokes.punchline: Violent imagery as a sincere compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect child's perspective on hairstyle with vivid imagery

jokes.text: I'm actually in fourth grade, but I read at a second grade level. They think I might have a problem.
jokes.setup: Shauna calling Bart sharp for a second grader
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact admission of academic struggles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - self-deprecating honesty delivered with innocent confidence

jokes.text: Extracting Lunch Money from a Cashless Society. We're gonna rob the guy giving the seminar.
jokes.punchline: Plan to rob the very person teaching about theft
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic escalation - going from learning about theft to immediate application

jokes.text: We're gonna rob the guy giving the seminar.
jokes.setup: Following up on the seminar joke
jokes.punchline: The simple, direct criminal plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that reveals the bullies' straightforward criminal logic

jokes.text: That's weird, my armpits are getting wet!
jokes.punchline: Surprised discovery of basic bodily function (sweating)
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness to normal physical responses

jokes.text: Seen it. Seen it. Seen it. British version was better. Totally scripted. Sued it.
jokes.punchline: Escalating reasons for dismissing shows, ending with absurd 'sued it'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation satirizing modern streaming culture and viewer cynicism

jokes.text: Well, islands are always entertaining, from Gilligan to Iwo Jima.
jokes.setup: Homer deciding to watch show about island
jokes.punchline: Comparing sitcom to WWII battle site as equally 'entertaining'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark juxtaposition showing Homer's warped perspective on entertainment

jokes.text: That flashback raised more questions than it answered.
jokes.punchline: Common viewer complaint about confusing narrative structure
jokes.explanation: Relatable meta-commentary on confusing television narratives

jokes.text: Purr-gatory. Where are we?!
jokes.setup: Discovering cat in coconut with flight number
jokes.punchline: Pun combining 'purr' and 'purgatory' for existential crisis
jokes.explanation: Clever pun that works on multiple levels - cat sound plus theological concept

jokes.text: And I'm sure everything they said will pay off handsomely!
jokes.setup: Homer enthusiastic about TV show's opening
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic prediction about show's plot resolution (clearly referencing Lost)
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of Lost's reputation for unresolved mysteries

jokes.text: I didn't steal nothing. If anything happens to her, I'll beat you up from inside your brain.
jokes.punchline: Jimbo's impossible but menacing threat about mental violence
jokes.explanation: Creative threat that's both menacing and absurd, perfect for teenage bully character

jokes.text: I use it all the time. ♪ Mi-mi-mi-mi-mi ♪
jokes.punchline: Awkward singing to 'prove' he uses perfume
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing Bart's awkward attempt to cover for Shauna

jokes.text: Isn't that what used to be the Beanie Baby store? Shut up.
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent observation about former store use, guard's irritated response
jokes.explanation: Nice period reference showing mall's economic decline

jokes.text: I'd use my legs but I've forgotten how!
jokes.setup: Homer trapped/unable to move on treadmill
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim about forgetting basic motor function
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor showing Homer's helplessness with technology

jokes.text: Hey, the first time we lost Maggie was in this store!
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to losing baby, implying it happened multiple times
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about parental negligence delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: More breast? What-- No! I'm offering you a chicken breast, you boob!
jokes.punchline: Double meaning creates awkward moment, resolved with clarification and ironic insult
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with family dinner awkwardness

jokes.text: Doesn't anyone here realize I'm only ten years old?
jokes.setup: Bart dealing with inappropriate situations all episode
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall to point out absurdity of his romantic subplot
jokes.explanation: Smart meta joke acknowledging the inappropriate nature of the plotline

jokes.text: I'm just not ready for cookies!
jokes.setup: Bart refusing dessert after dinner discussion
jokes.punchline: Treating cookies as if they're a serious life milestone
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist extension of the awkward dinner situation

jokes.text: I guess the weight will all come off at once, some day in the future.
jokes.setup: Observing Homer's lack of exercise results
jokes.punchline: Marge's naive optimism about delayed weight loss
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Marge's eternal optimism about Homer

jokes.text: His first word was a lie. Who did this? Mommy. She did?! Then I'm gonna withhold affection without telling her why.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining Homer's dishonesty from birth
jokes.punchline: Baby Homer's immediate passive-aggressive response
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character origin joke showing Homer's dysfunction from infancy - perfect escalation and character integration

jokes.text: Don't believe anything you were told by me in the past, because that was not me. But this is me now. But in less than a second, I'll be gone forever. I might be back!
jokes.punchline: Philosophical paradox about identity and time delivered rapidly
jokes.explanation: Smart parody of Lost-style confusing metaphysical dialogue

jokes.text: Now, if the Korean couple can't speak English, why were they doing a crossword puzzle where the answer to 23 down was 'enigma' spelled backwards?
jokes.setup: Homer analyzing TV show plot holes
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and nerdy continuity complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of obsessive fan analysis with specific absurd detail

jokes.text: And where will I watch TV? On the TV! And where will I walk?! On the ground!
jokes.punchline: Marge's exasperated obvious answers to Homer's rhetorical questions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing marital frustration through obvious statements

jokes.text: This is the stupidest fight ever! We've had stupider! I don't think so.
jokes.punchline: Parents immediately starting new stupid fight about previous stupid fights
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta escalation - the fight about fighting becoming another fight

jokes.text: This is the stupidest fight ever! We've had stupider! I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Lisa commenting on her parents' argument
jokes.punchline: Parents immediately arguing about their argument quality
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor where the characters become self-aware of their dysfunction

jokes.text: Hey, Fart-tholomew.
jokes.punchline: Crude pun on Bart's name combined with formal suffix
jokes.explanation: Simple but character-appropriate crude humor

jokes.text: Hey, Fart-tholomew.
jokes.punchline: Crude name-play on Bartholomew
jokes.explanation: Basic juvenile humor appropriate for the bully character

jokes.text: What any boy would: push you in a puddle and run away.
jokes.setup: Jimbo asking what Bart wants to do with Shauna
jokes.punchline: Innocent childhood interaction as romantic activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - age-appropriate response to inappropriate situation

jokes.text: Shauna, I forgot to grope you goodbye.
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to inappropriate physical contact as routine
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate but crude humor showing Jimbo's personality

jokes.text: Can it wait another 104 hours?
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting romance while kids are away
jokes.punchline: Homer's oddly specific time calculation to avoid intimacy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's TV addiction over romance

jokes.text: Oh, that's such a first-take answer!
jokes.setup: Marge explaining she's mad about door slamming
jokes.punchline: Homer dismissing real emotion as lazy TV writing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke about Homer applying TV criticism to real life emotions

jokes.text: Well, who wears a wool hat to a pool?
jokes.setup: Jimbo saying Bart doesn't respect him
jokes.punchline: Bart's logical fashion critique
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about character's signature look

jokes.text: It's not fair! You know that's hilarious!
jokes.punchline: Reluctant admission that his humiliation was funny
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing even bullies can appreciate humor at their expense

jokes.text: I'm on the grass apron! Huh? Ugh, everyone's a swimming pool lawyer.
jokes.punchline: Lifeguard's exasperation with technical rule interpretations
jokes.explanation: Great phrase 'swimming pool lawyer' - perfectly captures annoying rule-splitters

jokes.text: Why if it isn't Jimbo, Dorf and Ernie.
jokes.punchline: Marge getting their names wrong in overly polite greeting
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Marge's polite nature with intimidating teens

jokes.text: Why do men always think with their tongue?
jokes.punchline: Malapropism mixing body parts in common expression
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay showing character's confusion about anatomy and expression

jokes.text: This isn't, like, some creepy place under an off-ramp?
jokes.setup: Bart leading her to secret location
jokes.punchline: Specific and accurate fear about where nerds might gather
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail about stereotypical nerd hangout locations

jokes.text: All-seeing? Yeah, when's the last time you saw your feet?
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy claiming to be all-seeing
jokes.punchline: Bart's fat joke about his inability to see past his belly
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/punchline with good economy and character dynamics

jokes.text: You're a bully? A cyber bully. Anything I do, it is important that I can lie down while doing it.
jokes.setup: Revealing Comic Book Guy is also a bully
jokes.punchline: Modern lazy version of bullying that fits his sedentary lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - cyber bullying as laziest form of bullying for laziest character

jokes.text: You just got yourself in a flame war... with a teenage girl. Oh, flame on!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy preparing to cyber-bully
jokes.punchline: Fantastic Four reference while starting online fight
jokes.explanation: Good character-appropriate pop culture reference used in context

jokes.text: You have cloven me from my ponytail to my slipper shoe!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy losing online argument
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic medieval language for internet defeat
jokes.explanation: Great character voice - pompous dramatic language for mundane internet loss

jokes.text: 20 pound bricks are a dollar.
jokes.setup: Offering to sell old comic books
jokes.punchline: Selling comics by weight rather than content value
jokes.explanation: Good detail about devalued collectibles sold as bulk material

jokes.text: We discuss why airplane go crash-crash but people don't die-die.
jokes.setup: Apu explaining what the discussion group covers
jokes.punchline: Childish language for complex metaphysical concepts
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate simplification of complex TV show themes

jokes.text: the entire Strandiverse, is a piece of gravel in a cosmic kitty litter box!
jokes.punchline: Absurdly reductive cosmic metaphor for show's universe
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist metaphor that deflates pretentious sci-fi concepts

jokes.text: Now, I'm going to give you a spoiler it's not great right now.
jokes.setup: Homer angry about show being spoiled
jokes.punchline: Threatening to spoil their marriage with current assessment
jokes.explanation: Clever application of spoiler concept to real relationship problems

jokes.text: Now, I'm going to give you a spoiler it's not great right now.
jokes.setup: Homer angry about having his show spoiled
jokes.punchline: Threatening to spoil their relationship
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor applying TV spoiler logic to real relationships

jokes.text: You'd have to be a Navy SEAL to get in or out.
jokes.punchline: Military comparison for escaping teenage bullies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comparing kid's problem to military operation

jokes.text: And your brother's not a wad, he's a dink on the road to doofus.
jokes.punchline: Escalating insults in alliterative progression
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with alliteration and insult progression from Lisa

jokes.text: Your older sister's right, Bart.
jokes.punchline: Shauna not realizing Lisa is much younger
jokes.explanation: Good irony with Shauna misunderstanding family dynamics

jokes.text: Whoa, you should write that down, man. I'll remember it.
jokes.setup: Jimbo saying something he thinks is clever
jokes.punchline: Overconfidence in memory despite pattern of forgetting
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing character's inability to learn from mistakes

jokes.text: And that's something only an inappropriately older man can tell me.
jokes.punchline: Casually referencing inappropriate relationship as solution
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about teenage girls' misguided romantic choices

jokes.text: If I wrote down everything you told me to write down, I'd have no time for punching.
jokes.setup: Discussion about remembering vs. writing things down
jokes.punchline: Character's priorities perfectly clear - punching over note-taking
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - bully's time management prioritizing violence over productivity

jokes.text: I'd tell my best friend, but that's Marge too.
jokes.setup: Homer frustrated with Marge ruining his show
jokes.punchline: Realizing his wife is also his only friend
jokes.explanation: Sweet and funny realization about Homer's limited social circle and dependence on Marge

jokes.text: Purr-plexing, isn't it?
jokes.punchline: Callback to earlier 'Purr-gatory' pun with different word
jokes.explanation: Decent callback pun that bookends the TV watching storyline

tmdb_overview: Bart ends up in trouble with school bullies after Jimbo's girlfriend develops feelings for him; Homer buys a treadmill equipped with a TV.
